name: Permission-Based Opener
summary: An outreach or cold-calling opener that asks for a small amount of permission before continuing, reducing resistance through honesty and respect.
whenToUseIt: Use permission-based openers on the phone or in direct outreach when you want the buyer to feel in control of the interaction instead of trapped by it.
steps: Acknowledge that the interaction is unexpected or interruptive.
Ask for a very small amount of permission or time to continue.
Use that permission well by getting to relevance immediately.
example: A rep says, 'Can I take 30 seconds to tell you why I called, and then you can decide if it is worth continuing?' which often lowers guard faster than a scripted opener.
